name: Stan Getz
abstract: Stan Getz (born Stanley Gayetski; February 2, 1927 – June 6, 1991) was an American jazz saxophonist. Playing primarily the tenor saxophone, Getz was known as "The Sound" because of his warm, lyrical tone, with his prime influence being the wispy, mellow timbre of his idol, Lester Young.

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: I was 20 when I joined Stan Getz. ... I was playing with Stan Getz, and the drummer at the time was Joe Hunt... Stan one day said, 'I ran into Roy Haynes on the street today, and talked him into joining the band.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I had heard the bands of Woody Herman... It was one of the first musical solos I learned and I can still sing it. It was a solo played by Stan Getz on “Early Autumn”
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: And he called me up for a gig -- Mr. Stan Getz, and Elvin Jones, Wynton Elmer, Joe Kelly and myself, and we were working ... at Birdland.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: René Thomas who is a Belgian guitar player that worked with Stan Getz, who I would enjoy listening to thirty, forty years ago
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: But I had met Stan Getz before that... That's where I first met him, was at the Swing Club.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I had never met Stan Getz and he came up and asked me... And he was so nice to me
